questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.answer: Babur was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India.

questions.question: The Mughals were descendants of which two great lineages?
questions.answer: Genghis Khan (from the mother's side) and Timur (from the father's side).

questions.question: In which battle did Babur defeat Ibrahim Lodi?
questions.answer: The First Battle of Panipat in 1526.

questions.question: Which Mughal Emperor was defeated by Sher Shah Suri and forced to flee to Iran?
questions.answer: Humayun.

questions.question: Who was the regent of Akbar when he became emperor at the age of 13?
questions.answer: Bairam Khan.

questions.question: What was the 'Mansabdari' system?
questions.answer: A grading system used by the Mughals to fix rank, salary, and military responsibilities.

questions.question: What does the term 'Mansab' mean?
questions.answer: It means a position or rank.

questions.question: What was 'Zat' in the Mansabdari system?
questions.answer: A numerical value that determined the rank and salary of a mansabdar.

questions.question: What were the 'Jagirs'?
questions.answer: Land grants given to mansabdars from which they collected revenue as their salary.

questions.question: Who was Akbar’s revenue minister?
questions.answer: Todar Mal.

questions.question: What was 'Zabt'?
questions.answer: The revenue system based on the careful survey of crop yields and prices over a 10-year period.

questions.question: Who wrote the 'Akbar Nama'?
questions.answer: Abul Fazl.

questions.question: What is the third volume of Akbar Nama called?
questions.answer: Ain-i Akbari.

questions.question: What does 'Sulh-i Kul' mean?
questions.answer: It means 'universal peace', a dynamic idea of religious tolerance promoted by Akbar.

questions.question: What was the 'Ibadat Khana' used for?
questions.answer: It was a building where Akbar held religious discussions with scholars of different faiths.

questions.question: Who was the Mughal Emperor after Jahangir?
questions.answer: Shah Jahan.

questions.question: Which Mughal Emperor was the last of the 'Great Mughals' and died in 1707?
questions.answer: Aurangzeb.

questions.question: What title did Nur Jahan receive after marrying Jahangir?
questions.answer: Nur Jahan (Light of the World); her original name was Mehrunnisa.

questions.question: What were 'Subas'?
questions.answer: The provinces into which the Mughal Empire was divided.

questions.question: Who was the head of a 'Suba'?
questions.answer: The Subadar.

questions.question: Who were the 'Zamindars' in the Mughal period?
questions.answer: Local headmen or powerful chieftains who acted as intermediaries in collecting revenue.

questions.question: Which Sikh Guru was executed during the reign of Aurangzeb?
questions.answer: Guru Tegh Bahadur.

questions.question: What was the 'Sawar' rank in the Mansabdari system?
questions.answer: It indicated the number of cavalrymen (horsemen) a mansabdar was required to maintain.

questions.question: Which city did Shah Jahan build as his new capital?
questions.answer: Shahjahanabad (in Delhi).

questions.question: Why did the Mughals emphasize their Timurid ancestry over their Mongol ancestry?
questions.answer: Because the Mongols (Genghis Khan) were associated with the massacre of many people, while Timur had captured Delhi in 1398.
